skill.name: cc-skill-backend-patterns
skill.description: Backend architecture patterns, API design, database optimization, and server-side best practices for Node.js, Express, and Next.js API routes.
skill.summary: Apply backend API, database, caching, auth, rate limiting, queue, and logging patterns.
skill.author: affaan-m

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are contained in Markdown documentation examples, not executable skill code. No prompt injection, install hook, obfuscated payload, credential exfiltration path, or malicious network behavior was found. Risk is low because users may copy backend snippets involving JWT secrets, logging, and rate limiting without adapting them.

low_findings.title: Documentation Examples Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick patterns are Markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals in backend examples. They do not execute shell commands as part of the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are within Markdown TypeScript examples or code fence delimiters. There is no script file, command runner, or runtime path in the package.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access Is an Example Only
low_findings.description: The JWT secret reference appears in a token validation example. It is a normal backend pattern and does not read or transmit marketplace user secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The access is inside a documented TypeScript snippet for JWT verification. No executable package code or outbound network sink uses the value.

low_findings.title: Prototype Manipulation Finding Is Standard Error Subclassing
low_findings.description: Object.setPrototypeOf is used to repair the prototype chain for a custom ApiError class. This is not prototype pollution because no attacker-controlled property is assigned.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The target and prototype are fixed class objects in the example. The pattern does not merge untrusted objects or modify Object.prototype.

low_findings.title: Network and Weak Crypto Heuristics Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The network findings refer to array operations in an in-memory rate limiter. The weak cryptography findings match words such as API design and includes, not weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations do not contain HTTP client calls or cryptographic algorithms. They are front matter text, permission checks, and Map operations in examples.

content.value_statement: Backend teams need consistent patterns for APIs, data access, errors, auth, and operations. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical backend structures to adapt.
actual_capabilities: Explains RESTful API resource structure and query parameter conventions.
Provides repository and service layer patterns for backend separation.
Shows database optimization examples, including selective queries and batch loading.
Covers caching, retry, centralized error handling, and background job patterns.
Includes authentication, authorization, rate limiting, and structured logging examples.
limitations: It is guidance documentation, not an executable backend framework.
Examples need adaptation for each database, auth provider, and deployment target.
The in-memory rate limiter example is not suitable for distributed production systems.
Security controls are illustrative and still require project-specific review.

use_cases.title: Plan an API Service
use_cases.description: Create a resource-based API shape with service and repository boundaries before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review Backend Structure
use_cases.description: Compare an existing Node.js or Next.js backend against common patterns for errors, auth, caching, and logging.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Guidance
use_cases.description: Use the patterns as a shared reference for consistent backend design decisions across projects.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Backend Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this backend patterns skill to recommend a simple architecture for a Node.js API with users, projects, and tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor an API Route
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to refactor my API route into controller, service, and repository responsibilities. Explain each change.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Reliability Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my backend for error handling, retries, rate limiting, structured logging, and cache invalidation gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Backend
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design backend patterns for a production Next.js API with JWT auth, Supabase data access, Redis caching, and background jobs.

output_examples.input: Design an API for markets and positions.
output_examples.output: A resource model with market and position endpoints, repository boundaries, service methods, validation points, and pagination guidance.

output_examples.input: Review my backend error handling plan.
output_examples.output: A structured checklist covering expected errors, unexpected failures, response formats, logging context, and retry decisions.
Recommendations to select only needed fields, batch related records, add cache-aside reads, and avoid repeated per-row lookups.

best_practices: Adapt examples to your actual framework, database driver, and deployment topology.
Keep auth, validation, business logic, and data access in clear layers.
Review copied security snippets before using them in production.
anti_patterns: Do not copy sample secrets, token handling, or logging logic without a security review.
Do not use in-memory queues or rate limits when requests run across multiple servers.
Do not add every pattern when a simpler backend design meets the requirement.

faq.question: Does this skill create a backend application?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture and implementation patterns that an assistant can apply to your project.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it target?
faq.answer: The examples focus on Node.js, Express-style APIs, Next.js API routes, Supabase, and Redis-style caching.

faq.question: Can it help with database performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers selective queries, batching, N+1 prevention, transactions, and cache-aside reads.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for JWT validation, role-based access control, authorization checks, and rate limiting.

faq.question: Is the sample rate limiter production ready?
faq.answer: No. The in-memory example is useful for learning, but distributed systems need shared storage or managed rate limiting.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
